name: Taitung County (Taiwan)

colors.symbolism: purity, peace, and the bright future of the county
the Pacific Ocean that forms the eastern coast of the county
the eastern slopes of the Central Mountain Range and the rift valley
the warm sun of the eastern coast and the abundance of the harvest

description: A white field charged at the centre with a stylised multicolour emblem combining mountain, sea and sun motifs, with the characters 臺東 (Taitung).
history: Taitung County (Taidong Xian) occupies a long stretch of Taiwan's east coast and the East Rift Valley between the Central Mountain Range and the Coastal Range. The county is home to a large indigenous population. The current flag uses an emblem evoking the eastern coast, mountains, sun and the characters 臺東 ('eastern terrace').

features.symbolism: stylised motif of mountains, sun and sea with the characters 臺東 ('eastern terrace'), evoking the East Rift Valley, the Pacific coast, and the indigenous heritage of eastern Taiwan
